entry.title: Azure AI Machine Learning
entry.description: Azure AI Machine Learning provides cloud-based tools for building, training, and deploying machine learning models at scale.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes limited compute hours and storage for experimentation and small workloads.
limits: 60 free hours/month of standard compute instance
caveats: Compute and storage overages billed at standard rates
Some advanced features require paid tiers
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if usage exceeds free tier limits

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal and create a Machine Learning workspace.
Launch the Azure Machine Learning Studio.
Create a compute instance within your workspace.
Upload your data and start a new notebook or experiment.
Train and deploy your model using built-in tools.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need managed infrastructure for ML experiments, training, and deployment with easy scaling and collaboration.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require fully offline, on-premise ML workflows or need unlimited compute without cost concerns.
entry.body: Azure AI Machine Learning offers a managed environment for building, training, and deploying machine learning models, with a free tier suitable for experimentation and small projects.

entry.title: Cisco Networking Academy
entry.description: Cisco Networking Academy offers free online courses in networking, cybersecurity, and IT essentials for learners worldwide.

freeTierDetails.summary: Access free self-paced courses on networking, cybersecurity, and IT basics.
limits: Some advanced courses may require payment
caveats: Instructor-led courses may not be free

quickstartSteps: Visit https://skillsforall.com/ and create a free account.
Browse available courses and select one that matches your interests.
Enroll in the chosen course and start the self-paced modules.
Complete quizzes and hands-on labs as you progress.
Download completion certificates if available after finishing the course.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you want to learn networking, cybersecurity, or IT skills through free, self-paced online courses.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced, instructor-led training or free certification exams, as these may require payment.
avoidIf: you require accredited certification for free
entry.body: Cisco Networking Academy provides free, self-paced online courses in networking, cybersecurity, and IT, suitable for beginners and those preparing for certifications.

entry.title: DevNet Academy
entry.description: DevNet Academy offers free online courses and resources for learning networking, DevOps, and Cisco technologies.

freeTierDetails.summary: Access to all courses and learning resources is free.

quickstartSteps: Go to https://devnet-academy.com/
Sign up for a free account using your email.
Browse available courses and select one to start.
Begin the course and follow the modules at your own pace.
entry.whenToUse: Use DevNet Academy to learn networking, DevOps, or Cisco technologies at your own pace for free.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require official certification exams, advanced instructor-led training, or non-networking IT topics.
entry.body: DevNet Academy provides free, self-paced online courses focused on networking, DevOps, and Cisco technologies, making it suitable for beginners and those preparing for certifications.

entry.title: Django-tutorial.dev
entry.description: Interactive, free online tutorials for learning Django web development from scratch.

freeTierDetails.summary: Completely free access to all Django tutorials and interactive exercises.

useCases: Quick Django onboarding for new developers
quickstartSteps: Visit https://django-tutorial.dev.
Select a tutorial or lesson from the homepage.
Read the instructions and complete interactive exercises.
Advance through lessons at your own pace.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you want to learn Django fundamentals interactively without setup or cost.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced Django topics, certifications, or instructor-led training.
entry.body: Django-tutorial.dev offers free, interactive Django tutorials ideal for beginners looking to learn web development basics without setup or payment.

entry.title: edX
entry.description: edX offers free online courses from top universities and organizations across a wide range of subjects.

freeTierDetails.summary: Access to most course materials is free; verified certificates and some programs require payment.
limits: Free access to course videos, readings, and discussions
No certificate or graded assignments without payment
caveats: Graded assignments and certificates require payment
Some courses or programs may be paywalled

useCases: Test-driving a course before purchasing a certificate
quickstartSteps: Visit the edX website and create a free account.
Browse or search for a course of interest.
Enroll in a course with the 'Audit' (free) option.
Start learning by accessing course videos and materials.
entry.whenToUse: Use edX to access high-quality university courses for free when you don't need a certificate or graded assignments.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require official certification, graded assignments, or access to professional programs without paying.
avoidIf: you want full access to all course features for free
entry.body: edX provides free access to a wide range of university-level courses, allowing users to audit content without payment, but certificates and some features require a fee.

entry.title: FreeCodeCamp
entry.description: FreeCodeCamp offers free, interactive coding tutorials and certifications for web development, data science, and programming fundamentals.

freeTierDetails.summary: All courses, tutorials, and certifications are completely free.

quickstartSteps: Visit https://www.freecodecamp.org/ and create a free account.
Choose a certification path or course to start learning.
Work through interactive lessons and coding challenges.
Join the community forums for help or collaboration.
Complete projects to earn certifications.
entry.whenToUse: Use FreeCodeCamp to learn programming or web development at your own pace with free, structured courses and hands-on projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need instructor-led training, advanced niche topics, or formal accreditation recognized by employers.
entry.body: FreeCodeCamp is a nonprofit platform providing free, self-paced coding tutorials and certifications in web development, data science, and programming fundamentals, ideal for beginners and self-learners.

entry.title: Full Stack Open
entry.description: A free online course covering modern web development with JavaScript, React, Node.js, GraphQL, and TypeScript.

freeTierDetails.summary: Completely free access to all course materials and exercises.
caveats: Certificate only for those who submit exercises

useCases: Learning modern full stack web development
quickstartSteps: Visit the Full Stack Open website.
Read the course overview and prerequisites.
Start with Part 0 and follow the instructions.
Optionally, create a GitHub account to submit exercises.
Join the course Discord for community support.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you want a comprehensive, hands-on introduction to modern web development using JavaScript, React, and Node.js.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced backend topics, non-JavaScript stacks, or prefer video-based learning.
entry.body: Full Stack Open is a free, university-backed online course focusing on modern JavaScript-based web development, including hands-on projects and exercises.

entry.title: LabEx
entry.description: LabEx offers interactive cloud-based coding labs and courses for learning programming and DevOps skills.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier provides access to a limited set of labs and courses.
caveats: Some advanced labs and features require a paid plan

quickstartSteps: Go to https://labex.io and sign up for a free account.
Browse the available free labs and select one to start.
Follow the interactive instructions in the browser-based environment.
Complete the lab and review your results.
Explore additional free courses or upgrade for more content.
entry.whenToUse: Use LabEx to quickly practice coding or DevOps skills in a browser without local setup or when exploring new technologies.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need unlimited lab access, advanced content, or certifications without a paid plan.
avoidIf: seeking full course catalogs for free
entry.body: LabEx provides browser-based interactive labs and courses for learning programming and DevOps, with a free tier suitable for beginners and casual learners.

entry.title: MIT OpenCourseWare
entry.description: MIT OpenCourseWare offers free access to MIT course materials across a wide range of academic subjects.

freeTierDetails.summary: All course materials are available for free with no registration required.

quickstartSteps: Visit the MIT OpenCourseWare website.
Browse or search for a course of interest.
Select a course to view available materials.
Download lecture notes, assignments, or videos as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you want free, high-quality academic materials for self-study or to supplement formal education.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need interactive instruction, official certification, or academic credit.
entry.body: MIT OpenCourseWare provides free access to a vast library of MIT course materials, including lecture notes, assignments, and videos, for self-paced learning.

entry.title: Roadmap.sh
entry.description: Roadmap.sh offers curated developer learning paths, guides, and resources for various tech roles and skills.

freeTierDetails.summary: All roadmaps, guides, and most resources are free to access.
caveats: Premium features or early access may require payment

useCases: Finding curated resources for new skills
quickstartSteps: Visit https://roadmap.sh.
Browse available roadmaps by role or technology.
Select a roadmap to view recommended skills and resources.
Follow links to guides, tools, and community discussions.
Optionally, create a free account to track progress.
entry.whenToUse: Use Roadmap.sh to plan your learning journey or explore required skills for a specific tech role.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need hands-on coding environments or accredited certification, as Roadmap.sh is informational only.
entry.body: Roadmap.sh provides free, community-driven roadmaps and guides to help developers and teams navigate learning paths for various technical roles and skills.

entry.title: The Odin Project
entry.description: A free, open-source curriculum for learning web development from scratch, including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and full-stack skills.

freeTierDetails.summary: Completely free access to all curriculum, resources, and community support.

quickstartSteps: Visit theodinproject.com and create a free account.
Choose a curriculum path (e.g., Full Stack JavaScript).
Follow the introduction and complete the prerequisites.
Start the first module and join the Discord community for support.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you want a structured, self-paced path to learn web development for free, with community support and open-source resources.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need formal certification, instructor-led courses, or advanced specialization outside web development.
entry.body: The Odin Project offers a comprehensive, open-source curriculum for learning web development, covering everything from HTML and CSS to full-stack JavaScript, with a strong community and no cost.

entry.title: W3Schools
entry.description: W3Schools offers free interactive tutorials and references for web development languages and technologies.

freeTierDetails.summary: All core tutorials and references are available for free without registration.
caveats: Certification and ad-free experience require payment

useCases: Learning HTML, CSS, or JavaScript basics
Quick reference for syntax and examples
quickstartSteps: Visit https://www.w3schools.com/
Select a tutorial or reference topic from the homepage.
Read through lessons and try code examples in the online editor.
Use the search bar to find specific topics or functions.
entry.whenToUse: Use W3Schools for quick learning or referencing web development basics and trying code interactively in your browser.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced, in-depth tutorials, backend frameworks, or official documentation for complex libraries.
entry.body: W3Schools provides free, beginner-friendly tutorials and references for web technologies, featuring interactive code editors and concise explanations.
